rankings.title: Second Home Caps and Local Labor Markets — RDD at the 20% Threshold (Lex Weber)
conditions: verifying legal “bindingness” at 20% in practice by year
explicitly handling anticipation/transition periods—e.g.
exclude 2012–2013 or model event-time
confirming the second-home-share measure is pre-determined
consistently measured across municipalities

rankings.title: Do Municipal Mergers Save Money? RDD Evidence from Swiss Merger Referendums
conditions: securing comprehensive referendum microdata
defining the forcing variable rigorously for “all-must-pass” structure
building a consistent panel on stable geographic units—e.g.
mapping all outcomes onto post-merger municipality boundaries

rankings.title: Does Democracy Scale? Municipal Population Thresholds and Governance Quality in Switzerland
conditions: verifying exact legal thresholds
the population measurement rule—census date vs annual register
collecting an “actual governance regime” panel to test for fuzziness
performing strong manipulation tests around the cutoff

rankings.title: Minimum Wages at the Border: Spatial RDD Evidence from Swiss Cantonal Minimum Wages
conditions: upgrading to a “difference-in-discontinuities” design—border discontinuity pre vs post within each border segment
documenting
controlling for canton-pair discontinuities
ensuring outcomes match the margin—ideally establishment-level employment/wages near the border rather than broad municipal aggregates
